title: Mudslide Recovery Act
plain_summary: The Mudslide Recovery Act creates a new grant program to help communities fix damage caused by mudslides that happen after wildfires. The program will be run by two government departments and will give money to states, local governments, fire departments, and nonprofit groups. They can apply for grants to help repair the damage in their areas. The government has set aside five million dollars each year from 2026 to 2032 to pay for this program.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Economic Development, Public Buildings, and Emergency Management.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Mudslide Recovery Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Mudslide Recovery Grant Program
sections.text: Within one hundred eighty days after this law is passed, two government departments must work together to create a test grant program. The Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Homeland Security will run this program through the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA). The program will give money to groups that compete for grants. These grants help fix damage caused by mudslides that happen after wildfires. Groups that want grants must send in applications. The two departments will decide when applications are due, how to submit them, and what information must be included. Several types of groups can apply for grants. States, Indian tribes, and their forestry agencies can apply. Local governments and fire departments can apply. Nonprofit organizations can also apply, including homeowner associations that help communities at risk of wildfires. In this law, the word State means any of the fifty states, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, and any other territory or possession of the United States. Congress approves spending five million dollars each year from twenty twenty six through twenty thirty two to pay for this program.
